meaning: n.[古生]鸭嘴龙(一种嘴像鸭嘴的恐龙)
examples: Nicknamed Dakota, the hadrosaur is one of only five naturally preserved dinosaur mummies ever discovered.
While the scratches told the scientists how the animal may have chewed, the lack of pit marks on the teeth suggested what the hadrosaur was eating.
The cause of hadrosaur cancer remains a mystery today, but dinosaur fossils are helping unlock secrets of diseases millions of years old. Who knows what else they'll reveal.
Studies of the skin from across the fossil show that the skin was thinner toward the flanks, between the tail and the hips, where other hadrosaur fossils have shown bite marks.
